note: Energy transition fuels are reshaping aviation, trucking, and marine sectors. SAF blends with jet fuel; renewable diesel substitutes for ULSD. Prices reveal mandate-driven demand vs voluntary corporate buyers.

sections.title: Sustainable Aviation Fuel · SAF
sections.subtitle: Drop-in jet fuel from waste oils, palm, or biomass — under aviation decarbonization mandates

products.name: SAF Malacca Straits
products.context: Asia's SAF benchmark, FOB Singapore. Captures sustainable aviation fuel pricing for Asia-Pacific airlines. Major buyers include Singapore Airlines, Cathay Pacific. Premium over conventional Jet A1 reflects compliance demand under EU ReFuelEU and IATA mandates.

products.name: SAF China
products.context: Chinese SAF pricing — relevant for Air China, China Eastern, and growing domestic biofuels industry. China's SAF mandate is voluntary but expanding. Spread vs Malacca Straits reflects feedstock availability.

sections.title: SAF Production Cost Models
sections.subtitle: HEFA pathway — what producers pay to make SAF from used cooking oil vs palm oil

products.name: Max SAF (UCO) Model A
products.context: Used cooking oil (UCO) to SAF cost model. Most environmentally credentialed feedstock — earns highest carbon credits. UCO supply is constrained (limited collection); pushes price up vs other pathways.

products.name: Max SAF (UCO) Model B
products.context: Alternative UCO model with different yield assumptions. The spread between Model A/B/C reveals process efficiency improvements over time.

products.name: Max SAF (Palm Oil) Model A
products.context: Palm oil pathway — cheaper feedstock than UCO but earns fewer carbon credits due to land-use concerns. Some jurisdictions (EU) restrict palm-based SAF eligibility for mandate compliance.

products.name: Max SAF (Palm Oil) Model C
products.context: Lower-cost palm oil model. Margin between cost models and SAF market price (~$2,947) reveals how profitable SAF production currently is.

sections.title: Renewable Diesel · HVO
sections.subtitle: Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil — drop-in diesel substitute, identical performance to ULSD

products.name: Class II Renewable Diesel
products.context: Renewable diesel meeting California LCFS Class II carbon intensity threshold. Used in trucking, off-road equipment, marine. Trades at premium vs ULSD due to LCFS credit value (~$80-120/credit).

products.name: Class IV Renewable Diesel
products.context: Higher-grade renewable diesel, lower carbon intensity (Class IV under LCFS). Earns more credits per gallon. Spread to Class II reflects market pricing of carbon intensity differential.
